questions.explanation: Objects are reference values — a and b point to the same object.
Spread creates a SHALLOW copy — a and b are independent.
Shallow copy — the nested object is still shared.

questions.prompt: Which produces a DEEP clone of an object?
questions.explanation: structuredClone is the built-in deep clone. JSON round-trip is a common older trick.
Object.freeze prevents modification (silent in non-strict mode).
=== on objects compares identity, not shape. Different objects → false.
Computed property names: [expr] evaluates the expression to a key.
fromEntries builds an object from [key, value] pairs — inverse of Object.entries.
